page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m4-vws.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, supported by 33 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 23.0 GB of system memory, providing 5.75 GB per core. For accelerated workloads, it includes a fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU allocation (0.25x) with 4 GB of dedicated VRAM. It does not feature local storage, meaning all storage must be network-attached, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.vgn6i-m4-vws.xlarge demonstrates entry-level performance profiles. Its stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU scores fall within the bottom 25% of tested servers, with values of 1103.98 and 2934.21 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 164.04 GB/sec, which drops to 19.82 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, placing it in the bottom 10%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also perform in the bottom 10%. For database operations, the instance achieves a PassMark database score of 1083.5 and handles 2.82 million Redis SET operations per second. Small-scale LLM inference on a 135M model yields 233.71 tokens per second for prompt processing and 64.28 tokens per second for text generation.

Given its fractional GPU allocation and modest CPU performance, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring GPU acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated GPU. The resource density is tailored for lightweight virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI), entry-level AI inference, basic video decoding, or development environments where a full GPU would remain underutilized. However, the lack of local storage and the lower baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps represent key tradeoffs for data-intensive or high-throughput applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m4-vws.xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server powered by 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 23.0 GB of RAM. It features a fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU (0.25x) with 4 GB of VRAM, making it a specialized option for lightweight accelerated tasks. The instance lacks local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place the CPU and memory bandwidth in the lower tiers, with a PassMark CPU score of 3597.24 and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth at 19.82 GB/sec. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that need GPU capabilities without the cost of a full accelerator. It is best suited for entry-level AI inference, lightweight virtual desktops, and development environments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m4-vws.xlarge is an x86_64 server with 4 Intel Xeon cores, 23 GB RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU with 4 GB VRAM, designed for entry-level accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m4-vws.xlarge features 4 Intel Xeon cores, 23 GB RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU for entry-level accelerated workloads.
tagline: An entry-level virtual server featuring fractional GPU acceleration and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for lightweight graphical and inference workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 cores at 2.5 GHz
Fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU (0.25x) with 4 GB VRAM
23.0 GB of system memory (5.75 GB per core)
No local storage with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suitable for lightweight AI inference and virtual desktops
